title: App User Guide
text: package contains number functions useful correcting sonde data, main part package Shiny app allows user interactively load, view, correct, export sonde data. app works creating modifying sonde project (sondeproj) object list containing sonde data, flags, metadata. See help(example_sondeproj) details project structure. app consists ten modules pages. put order represent typical correction workflow, steps need performed order. Load Data: Load either existing sonde project, load raw data metadata create sonde project, amend existing sonde project new data. Can also load download precipitation data include project. Visualize: Explore loaded data via interactive plot, view metadata data summary statistics. Can also view data versions remove periods sonde water. Data Checks: View summaries data gaps duplicates. Resolve duplicates add notes duplicate/missing data. Quality Flags: Flag data points questionable. flags can visualized plots across modules used automatically select points outlier removal. Physical Limits: Removes data points outside specified range. Limits default common sensor measurement limits. Outlier Removal: Remove outlier data points. Removal can done via combination automatic outlier detection manual addition removal points. Interpolation: Fill missing data points using interpolation. User can select several different interpolation methods can control big gap filled via interpolation. Shift Corrections: Perform shift correction selected set points apply linear drift correction specific file. Drift corrections based calibration checks provided. fDOM Corrections: Apply temperature turbidity corrections fDOM data using one several methods. Methods default published coefficient values can set custom values. Download Data: Generate view time summarized data; export data metadata; save sonde project.

title: App User Guide
text: step used correct fDOM data. fDOM measured using optical sensor affected quenching due temperature differences turbidity interference. determine “true” fDOM value without interferences, data must corrected. corrected fDOM signal shown red line top data.

title: App User Guide
text: Watras et al. 2011 proposed temperature correction equation : fDOMT=fDOM1+ρ(T−25) fDOM_{T} = \frac{fDOM}{1 + \rho (T - 25)} fDOMTfDOM_T temperature corrected fDOM, ρ\rho correction factor TT water temperature. correction factor known vary somewhat site. default 0.011 suggested Akie et al. 2024.

title: App User Guide
text: Turbidity corrections can quite complex number attempts devising equations correct turbidity. module currently supports five different correction options: None: Skip turbidity corrections, temperature, turbidity corrections thought site specific, particularly high turbidities. Inverse Polynomial: equation suggested Fleck et al. 2026 applies correction factor based quadratic equation turbidity. found fit similarly complex, five parameter models. Default values values reported Fleck et al. 2026. Exponential (1-parameter): One initial correction equations proposed Downing et al. 2012 uses single correction factor. default correction factor comes Akie et al. 2024 value generic Elliot silt loam suggested appropriate use turbidity values 300 FNU. Exponential (2-parameter): slightly modified version original 1-parameter model proposed Fleck et al. 2026. model works well lower turbidity values can poor fit higher turbidity values (>100-200 FNU). Default correction values come Fleck et al. 2026. Exponential (5-parameter): approach work across full turbidity range, Fleck et al. 2026 proposed 5-parameter model fit high turbidity data well, complex fit. Default correction values come Fleck et al. 2026. Similar modules, want save fDOM correction use Flag points section. add optional note save updated values.

what: Perform Quality Assurance and Data Corrections on Sonde Data
title: Perform Quality Assurance and Data Corrections on Sonde Data
text: Provides interactive workflow quality assurance quality control (QA/QC) water quality sonde data. package associated Shiny app enables users import raw sonde datasets, review correct observations, apply quality flags, visualize time-series data alongside associated metadata, summarize observations,export cleaned datasets metadata maintaining reproducible record edits.

title: Perform Quality Assurance and Data Corrections on Sonde Data
text: Continuous water quality data extremely useful due ’s high resolution, however high resolution, performing QA/QC can extremely challenging due number data points. Additionally, since sondes typically streams long stretches maintenance visits, data can extremely messy. make cleaning sonde data extremely time intensive challenging. tools exist help clean sonde data either expensive (Aquarius); limited correction capacity (ContDataQC); longer maintained (ODM Tools). Thus need free, open-source interactive tool. package developed part Wildfire Water Security Project.

User can select several different interpolation methods can control big gap filled via interpolation. Shift Corrections: Perform shift correction selected set points apply linear drift correction specific file. Data changes captured diff used track changes made data move data versions. log added change log (viewable Visualize tab) showing parameter, number points changed, step, user, notes change (e.g., limits used removal), user added notes.

title: Perform Quality Assurance and Data Corrections on Sonde Data
text: key principle data management reproducibility: able reproduce steps get raw dataset clean dataset. sonde data corrections laborious process requiring detailed notes change. SondePolishR app automates process. keeps descriptive record changes made addition creating diff file tracks specific changes made. diff files stored list within project. Within app can view data looks different versions Visualize tab selecting rows change log table. uses apply_diff() function can used outside app move raw data set cleaned dataset can used “undo” changes go cleaned data raw data.

title: Perform Quality Assurance and Data Corrections on Sonde Data
text: sonde project stored .RDS object easily useable R. However user friendly. Download Data tab offers number options generate clean, immediately usable data exports. Data frequency: Summarizes data commonly used intervals (e.g., hourly, weekly, annual). Summary method: Used determine summary method use (e.g., mean, median, minimum, max). addition data, user can also export metadata contained within project including: Duplicate gap tables added user notes Hourly precipitation data (download NASA power via nasapower package) Change log

what: SondePolishR: Perform Quality Assurance and Data Corrections on Sonde Data — SondePolishR-package
title: SondePolishR: Perform Quality Assurance and Data Corrections on Sonde Data — SondePolishR-package
text: Provides interactive workflow quality assurance quality control (QA/QC) water quality sonde data. package enables users import raw sonde datasets, review correct observations, apply quality flags, visualize time-series data alongside associated metadata, summarize observations, export cleaned datasets metadata maintaining reproducible record edits.

title: Shiny module to added flags and save changes to data — apply_edit_UI
text: id shiny ID action button sondeproj `reactiveVal` holding current `sondeproj`. edit `reactiveVal` holding list length six: - data: new updated data `data.frame` - rows: logical vector specifies rows changed TRUE - y_var: parameter edited - step: name editing step changelog - note: optional note add changelog - flag: character flag use edits data

text: changes `data.frame`s saved `diff` object, can used move changes made applying `diff` data.

title: Apply a diff object to a dataset — apply_diff
code: apply_diff(data, diff, id = "DateTime", invert = FALSE, skip_merge = TRUE)

title: Apply a diff object to a dataset — apply_diff
text: data data apply `diff` . Must contain columns `diff`. diff list `diff` single `diff` objects generated using `get_diff`. id name column name used match observations `olddata` `newdata`. invert logical. `TRUE` changes reversed. skip_merge logical. `TRUE` skip `diff` data merges.

title: Apply a diff object to a dataset — apply_diff
text: `data.frame` columns `data` changes `diff` applied. Note increase number rows diff data merge.

title: Apply a diff object to a dataset — apply_diff
code: data1 <- example_data[1:10,] data2 <- data1 data2$fDOM_QSU[1:4] <- NA diff <- get_diff(data1, data2)  #get new data from original newdata2 <- apply_diff(data1, diff) all.equal(data2, newdata2) #> [1] TRUE  #get orginal data from new data newdata1 <- apply_diff(data2, diff, invert=TRUE) all.equal(data1, newdata1) #> [1] TRUE

text: proj `sondeproj` object holding sonde data. dup_row Row outputs `identify_dups` keep_opt Character describing set duplicates keep (identified `DupNum`), "use_mean" take mean values, "remove_both" remove duplicated values. flag_notes Optional character additional notes write changelog

title: Deals with duplicates in data and documents changes — apply_dup_edits
text: `sondeproj` updated data, flags, changelog

title: Deals with duplicates in data and documents changes — apply_dup_edits
code: path <- file.path(fs::path_package("extdata", package = "SondePolishR"), "example-sondeproj-messy.RDS") proj <- readRDS(path) proj$duplicates <- identify_dups(proj$data) flagged <- apply_dup_edits(proj, proj$duplicates[1,], "use_mean")

title: Log edits to a `sondeproj` — apply_edit

title: Log edits to a `sondeproj` — apply_edit
Log edits to a `sondeproj` — apply_edit
text: proj `sondeproj` object holding sonde data. edit list length six: - data: new updated data `data.frame` - rows: logical vector specifies rows changed TRUE - y_var: parameter edited - step: name editing step changelog - note: optional note add changelog - flag: character flag use edits data - changetype: character specifying add flag, either "flag_rm", "flag_chg", "flag_add"

title: Log edits to a `sondeproj` — apply_edit
text: `sondeproj` object edits made.

title: Log edits to a `sondeproj` — apply_edit
code: data <- example_data data$fDOM_QSU[1:4] <- NA rows <- rep(FALSE, nrow(data)) rows[1:4] <- TRUE edit <- list(data = example_data,              rows = rows,              y_var = "fDOM_QSU",              step = "outlier removal",              note = "example edit",              flag = "RM07",              changetype = "flag_rm") updated_proj <- apply_edit(example_sondeproj, edit)

title: Apply fDOM temperature and turbidity corrections — correct_fdom
Apply fDOM temperature and turbidity corrections — correct_fdom
text: proj sondeproj object. temp Either NULL list object (see details) temperature correction. turb Either NULL list object (see details) turbidity correction.

title: Apply fDOM temperature and turbidity corrections — correct_fdom
text: data.frame object fDOM updated corrections.

title: Apply fDOM temperature and turbidity corrections — correct_fdom
text: apply correction must supply list object following structure: params: named list parameter values names match arguments within function fun: function apply fDOM data first argument fDOM, second either temperature turbidity, third parameters within function, pulled params.

title: Apply fDOM temperature and turbidity corrections — correct_fdom
code: temp <- list(params = list(rho = -0.011),              fun = function(fdom, temp, parms){fdom / (1 + parms$rho*(temp - 25))}) corr_data <- correct_fdom(example_sondeproj, temp=temp, turb=NULL)

Sample Calibration Check Data — example_calcheck
text: data.frame following columns: Date: date site visit. Site_Code: site name site code. Parameter: name parameter, match column names read_sonde. Resident_Probe_Serial: serial number sonde located site. Resident_Value: measured value parameter resident sonde. Check_Probe_Serial: serial number sonde used check calibration. freshly calibrated. Check_Value: measured value parameter check sonde. Notes: Notes associated calibration check data entry.

title: Sample Sonde Data — example_data
text: example output read_sonde function.

title: Sample Sonde Data — example_data
Sample Sonde Data — example_data
text: data.frame object 8071 rows 13 columns: Index: number, 1 number rows data.frame. Date: Date measurement form YYYY-MM-DD. Time_HH_mm_ss: Time measurement form hh:mm:ss. DateTime: Date time measurement POSIXct. DateTime_rd: Date time measurement POSIXct rounded nearest data interval value. Site_Name: Name site. Battery_V: Battery voltage measurement collected. fDOM_QSU: Fluorescent dissolved organic matter (fDOM) measured Quinine Sulfate Units (QSU). ODO_mg_L: Dissolved oxygen measured mg/L. pH: Dissolved oxygen measured pH units. SpCond_uS_cm: Specific conductivity measured µS/cm. Temp_C: Temperature measured degrees C. Turbidity_FNU: Turbidity measured Formazin Nephelometric Units (FNU).

title: Sample Sonde Data — example_data
text: Forest Ecohydrology Watershed Science Lab (2024) Fall Creek: 2024-07-31 2024-10-23. Data set.

title: Sample Field Form — example_fieldform
text: example field form used collect information field visit check perform maintenance. Critically information used track periods sonde water.

title: Sample Field Form — example_fieldform
Sample Field Form — example_fieldform
text: data.frame following columns: Date: date site visit. Site_Code: site name site code. Time: start time field visit (used guess water periods data missing). Start_Sonde_Serial: serial number sonde water start visit. Start_Sonde_Name: sonde name sonde water start visit. End_Sonde_Serial: serial number sonde water end visit. End_Sonde_Name: sonde name sonde water end visit. Removal_Time: time sonde removed water. Return_Time: time sonde returned water. Next_Timepoint: next timepoint data collected. next "good" time point sonde returned water, sometimes timepoint wiper checked sonde water. Data_Download: Logical, data downloaded visit? Download_Device: name device data downloaded . Remove_Period: Logical, data disruption merits removing data water period. Used skip periods coarse water periods (missing times), data appears uninterrupted prevent removing excess data. Crew: names initials people performing field visit. Weather: description weather field visit. Notes: Notes associated field visit. DataEntry_Notes: Additional notes data entry. Can name includes "Notes"

Sample Precipitation Data — example_precip
text: data.frame following columns: DateTime: date time observation. Precip_mm_hr: hourly precipitation value hour mm.

title: Sample Precipitation Data — example_precip
text: average MERRA-2 bias corrected total precipitation surface earth. NASA POWER Project. NASA Langley Research Center (LaRC), Prediction Worldwide Energy Resources (POWER), accessed 2026-06-26. https://power.larc.nasa.gov/parameters/?parameter=PRECTOTCORR

title: Sample Sonde Project — example_sondeproj
text: example sonde project, list type object stores data, associated metadata, tracks changes made data.

title: Sample Sonde Project — example_sondeproj
Sample Sonde Project — example_sondeproj
text: object class sondeproj following elements: meta: list length three: site: character sonde site name. tz: character site timezone. coords: vector length two latitude longitude site. data: data.frame dataset, updated changes. flags: list length four: flag_rm: data.frame measurement columns _flag appended name number rows. Used store flag values values marked questionable. flag_rm: data.frame measurement columns _flag appended name number rows. Used store flag values values removed. flag_chg: data.frame measurement columns _flag appended name number rows. Used store flag values values altered. flag_add: data.frame measurement columns _flag appended name number rows. Used store flag values values added (likely interpolation). precip: data.frame  precipitation values. fieldform: data.frame field form data. See example_fieldform details structure. calcheck: data.frame calibration check data. See example_calcheck details structure. diffs: list data_diff stores changes made data. See daff package details structure. changelog: data.frame stores summary changes made data_diff: datetime: date time change made. parameter:  parameter change made . step: name processing step change made . nchanged: number points changed. note: Notes added changes made. user: username person made change. diff_name: name associated data.diff.

title: Track differences between sonde data — get_diff
text: `diff` object named list item column tracked. list item either `NULL` changes column following structure: - op_type: character describing type change made - id: id values change made - new_data: values changed values `newdata` - old_data: values changed values `olddata`

Start_Sonde_Serial: serial number sonde water start visit. Start_Sonde_Name: sonde name sonde water start visit. End_Sonde_Serial: serial number sonde water end visit. End_Sonde_Name: sonde name sonde water end visit. Removal_Time: time sonde removed water. Return_Time: time sonde returned water. Next_Timepoint: next timepoint data collected. next "good" time point sonde returned water, sometimes timepoint wiper checked sonde water. Data_Download: Logical, data downloaded visit? Download_Device: name device data downloaded . Remove_Period: Logical, data disruption merits removing data water period. Used skip periods coarse water periods (missing times), data appears uninterrupted prevent removing excess data. Crew: names initials people performing field visit. Weather: description weather field visit. Notes: Notes associated field visit. DataEntry_Notes: Additional notes data entry. Can name includes "Notes"

title: Upload raw sonde data — read_sonde
code: read_sonde(   file,   return = "df",   encoding = NULL,   flags = FALSE,   skip = NULL,   tz = "Etc/GMT+8" )

title: Upload raw sonde data — read_sonde
text: file File path sonde data (.csv). return format data returned ? Either df list. encoding File encoding, guess NULL. flags Logical, TRUE flag column added parameter. skip number rows skip, assumes first row header,  guess NULL. tz Time zone file. specified use user timezone. See details information.

title: Upload raw sonde data — read_sonde
text: return df: data.frame containing date, time, site name, "core" measurements (available): Specific conductivity measured µS/cm. Fluorescent dissolved organic matter (fDOM) measured Quinine Sulfate Units (QSU). Dissolved oxygen measured mg/L. Turbidity measured Formazin Nephelometric Units (FNU). pH measured pH units. Temperature measured degrees C. Battery voltage measured volts. return list: list containing: serials: probe serial numbers associated measurement. data: sonde data formatted described .

title: Upload raw sonde data — read_sonde
text: Daylights saving time can cause issues continuous datasets, data often collected standard time. case use Etc/GMT offsets. See OlsonNames available timezones. Note Etc/GMT signs reverse UTC signs. example UTC-8 Etc/GMT+8, signifying Pacific Standard Time (PST).

title: Upload raw sonde data — read_sonde
code: file <- file.path(fs::path_package("extdata", package = "SondePolishR"), "example-csv-data1.csv") data <- read_sonde(file) #> Error in utf8::as_utf8(text): entry 9 has wrong Encoding; marked as "UTF-8" but invalid leading byte (0xB5) at position 68  file <- file.path(fs::path_package("extdata", package = "SondePolishR"), "sonde-usb-example.csv") data <- read_sonde(file, return = "list") #> Error in utf8::as_utf8(text): entry 1 has wrong Encoding; marked as "UTF-8" but invalid leading byte (0xFF) at position 1

title: Interpolate data gaps — run_interp
text: data_interp extra column added: yvar_fill: Filled values y_var.

title: Interpolate data gaps — run_interp
text: Currently follow interpolation options supported: linear: Uses zoo::na.approx() function linearly fill gaps. spline: Uses zoo::na.spline() function fill gaps via spline interpolation (makes smoother curves). random_forest: Depending much data project, option can little slow. Uses missForest::missForest() fill gaps. uses responses across available parameters try fill missing parameter. ts_interp: Uses forecast::na.interp() treats data time series make predictions based natural seasonal patterns within data. seasonality determined freq. instance, set 1, account daily fluctuations. set 365 look annual fluctuations.

title: Interpolate data gaps — run_interp
